sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The MOUSE click changed 3 cells. Looking at the image, the right green square now has a white center instead of black! So clicking on the green square toggles its center color from black to white. This is interesting - maybe the goal is to make both green squares have the same center color (white), matching the left green square's center.
Let me check the current state more carefully.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed - the right green square's center color changed
seg = current_frame.segmentation
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Right green area:")
for r in range(29, 34):
    if r < len(ascii_lines):
        print(f"{r}: {ascii_lines[r][40:50]}")
print("\nLeft green area:")
for r in range(28, 34):
    if r < len(ascii_lines):
        print(f"{r}: {ascii_lines[r][26:36]}")
# Check green square centers
print("\nGreen squares:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  bounds: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
